    • builderCleanGraph

      public void builderCleanGraph(org.eclipse.core.resources.IProject project, boolean onlyClean, ProgressManager manager) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • builderBuildGraph

      public void builderBuildGraph(ProgressManager manager) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      This method implements the incremental build for Rodin projects. The building process terminates when all tools (@see IAutomaticTool) have run and the the graph is stable. As dependencies are added, changed, or removed during the build, it can happen that the build would have been started with wrong dependencies, hence, the topological order would be invalid for the Rodin project. In this case the build is restarted, recreating all derived resources that may have been invalidated.
      Parameters:
      manager - The progress manager to use
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - If any problem occurred during build.

    • builderExtractNode

      public void builderExtractNode(Node node, ProgressManager manager) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • setInstable

      protected void setInstable()
      Mark the graph instable. This causes the topological sort to be repeated (and the tools to be re-run)
